heading: Posthumous citizenship through death while on active-duty service in armed forces during World War I, World War II, the Korean hostilities, the Vietnam hostilities, or in other periods of military hostilities
label: 1440–1 Posthumous citizenship through death while on active-duty service in armed forces during World War I, World War II, the Korean hostilities, the Vietnam hostilities, or in other periods of military hostilities
notes: Codification, referred to in subsec. (c)(2)(A)(i), was in the original “the date of enactment of this section”, which was translated as meaning the date of enactment of , which enacted subsec. (c) of this section, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.November 24, 2003Pub. L. 108–136

children.heading: Permitting granting of posthumous citizenship
children.label: a Permitting granting of posthumous citizenship
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, the Secretary of Homeland Security shall provide, in accordance with this section, for the granting of posthumous citizenship at the time of death to a person described in subsection (b) if the Secretary of Homeland Security approves an application for that posthumous citizenship under subsection (c).

children.heading: Noncitizens eligible for posthumous citizenship
children.label: b Noncitizens eligible for posthumous citizenship

children.text: served honorably in an active-duty status in the military, air, or naval forces of the United States during any period described in the first sentence of ,section 1440(a) of this title
died as a result of injury or disease incurred in or aggravated by that service, and
satisfied the requirements of clause (1) or (2) of the first sentence of .section 1440(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: upon locating the next-of-kin, and if so requested by the next-of-kin, by the Secretary of Defense or the Secretary’s designee with the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services in the Department of Homeland Security immediately upon the death of that person; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of the person’s death;

children.label: B
children.text: the request is accompanied by a duly authenticated certificate from the executive department under which the person served which states that the person satisfied the requirements of paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (b); and

children.label: C
children.text: the Director finds that the person satisfied the requirement of subsection (b)(3).

children.heading: Documentation of posthumous citizenship
children.label: d Documentation of posthumous citizenship
children.text: If the Director of the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services approves the request referred to in subsection (c), the Director shall send to the next-of-kin of the person who is granted citizenship, a suitable document which states that the United States considers the person to have been a citizen of the United States at the time of the person’s death.
